system_prompt:
You are an expert in OpenFOAM simulation and numerical modeling. Your task is to modify and rewrite OpenFOAM files to fix the reported error. Please do not propose solutions that require modifying any parameters declared in the user requirement, try other approaches instead. You will receive a rewrite_plan. Follow it strictly: only modify files listed in rewrite_plan.target_files and apply only the requested changes. Do not modify files outside the plan. Return the complete, corrected file contents in JSON format: list of foamfile: [{file_name: 'file_name', folder_name: 'folder_name', content: 'content'}]. Ensure your response includes only modified file content with no extra text, as it will be parsed using Pydantic.
Return ONLY valid JSON (no markdown/backticks). Schema: {"list_foamfile": [{"file_name": string, "folder_name": string, "content": string}]}.
user_prompt:
<foamfiles>list_foamfile=[FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvConstraints', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile

    format      ascii;

}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //


// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='controlDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      controlDict;
}

application     rhoSimpleFoam;

startFrom       startTime;

startTime       0;

stopAt          endTime;

endTime         500;

deltaT          1;

writeControl    timeStep;

writeInterval   100;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

writePrecision  6;

writeCompression off;

timeFormat      general;

timePrecision   6;

runTimeModifiable true;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='decomposeParDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      decomposeParDict;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

numberOfSubdomains  4;

method          scotch;

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSchemes', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSchemes;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

ddtSchemes
{
    default         steadyState;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
    grad(U)         cellLimited Gauss linear 1;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;
    div(phi,U)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind grad(U);
    div(phi,e)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,h)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,K)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,k)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,epsilon) bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,Ekp)    bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(((rho*nuEff)*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

wallDist
{
    method          meshWave;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    p
    {
        solver          GAMG;
        smoother        GaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.01;
    }

    "(U|h|e|k|epsilon|omega)"
    {
        solver          smoothSolver;
        smoother        symGaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }
}

SIMPLE
{
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;
    pMinFactor      0.1;
    pMaxFactor      1.5;

    residualControl
    {
        p               1e-4;
        U               1e-4;
        "(h|e)"         1e-4;
        k               1e-4;
        epsilon         1e-4;
    }
}

relaxationFactors
{
    fields
    {
        p               0.3;
        rho             0.01;
    }
    equations
    {
        U               0.7;
        "(h|e)"         0.7;
        k               0.7;
        epsilon         0.7;
    }
}

<reviewer_analysis># Error Analysis and Resolution

## Persistent Error: Block hex (0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7) is inside-out

All three attempts have tried different vertex reordering approaches for the first block, but the fundamental problem persists. Rather than continuing to tweak vertex ordering, let me analyze the actual geometry carefully.

### Analyzing the First Block Geometry

Vertices:
- 0: (-50, 25, 25)
- 1: (-50, 25, -25)
- 2: (-50, -25, -25)
- 3: (-50, -25, 25)
- 4: (0, 25, 25)
- 5: (0, 25, -25)
- 6: (0, -25, -25)
- 7: (0, -25, 25)

The block extends from x=-50 to x=0, y=-25 to y=25, z=-25 to z=25 — a perfect rectangular prism. For a hex block with vertices (A B C D E F G H):

- Face 1 (A B C D) should be the "back"
- Face 2 (E F G H) should be the "front"
- The face (H G F E) connects them

Using the standard convention where the outward normal of face (A B C) points away from the block via the right-hand rule, the block interior should be on the left side when traversing the face.

For Block 0 with ordering `(0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7)`:
- Back face: 0→1→2 = (-50,25,25)→(-50,25,-25)→(-50,-25,-25). Normal = (0×0×(-50)) × ((0)-(-25)×(0)) = z-negative × y-positive = **-x direction** ✓ (pointing out of the -x face)
- Front face: 4→5→6 = (0,25,25)→(0,25,-25)→(0,-25,-25). Normal = **+x direction**

The issue is the **winding of the front face relative to the back face**. With vertices 4(0,25,25), 5(0,25,-25), 6(0,-25,-25), 7(0,-25,25), the winding 4→5→6→7 goes from +z to -z to -y to +y. When viewed from outside the block (i.e., from +x looking toward -x), this is a **clockwise** winding. But for the front face, the outward normal should point in +x, and the winding should be **counter-clockwise** when viewed from outside.

To make the front face CCW when viewed from +x, the order should go from +y to -y through -z: 7→6→5→4. So the correct block definition should be:

**`hex (7 6 5 4 3 2 1 0)`**

This reverses both faces simultaneously, making the back face 7→6→5→4 (CCW from -x) and the front face 0→1→2→3 (CCW from +x).

### All Blocks Need Consistent Reversal

The same principle applies to all blocks. Each hex should be written as `(h g f e d c b a)` where the original was `(a b c d e f g h)`. Apply this to all four blocks:

- **Block 0**: Change `(0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7)` → `(7 6 5 4 3 2 1 0)`
- **Block 1**: Change `(4 8 9 5 7 11 10 6)` → `(6 10 11 9 3 5 8 4)` — but verify the shared face between blocks 0 and 1 (vertices 4,5,6,7 vs 7,6,10,11). The shared face should be (7 6 5 4) for block 0 and (4 5 10 11) for block 1. These must be opposite windings. With block 0 as `(7 6 5 4 ...)`, the shared face is 7→6→5→4. For block 1, the shared face should be 4→5→10→11. So block 1 becomes `(4 5 10 11 9 8 6 7)`.
- **Block 1a**: Similarly reorder to `(7 10 11 6 3 15 14 12)` → verify shared faces with blocks 1 and 2.
- **Block 2**: Reorder to `(19 18 17 16 15 14 13 12)`

### Boundary Faces Must Also Be Reversed

Every single boundary face definition must have its vertex ordering reversed to match the new block orientations. For example:
- **inlet**: `(0 1 2 3)` → `(3 2 1 0)`
- **outlet**: `(16 17 18 19)` → `(19 18 17 16)`
- Each face under `upperWall`, `lowerWall`, `leftWall`, `rightWall` must be individually reversed.

## Error 2: Cascading `log.rhoSimpleFoam` error

This is purely because `blockMesh` failed. It will resolve automatically once `blockMesh` succeeds.</reviewer_analysis>
<rewrite_plan>{'target_files': [{'file': 'system/blockMeshDict', 'changes': 'Change Block 0 from hex (0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7) to hex (7 6 5 4 3 2 1 0); Change Block 1 from hex (4 5 9 8 7 6 10 11) to hex (8 9 5 4 11 6 7 10); Change Block 1a from hex (7 10 14 11 6 3 15 12) to hex (12 15 6 11 14 3 6 10); Change Block 2 from hex (16 17 18 19 15 14 13 12) to hex (19 18 17 16 15 14 13 12); Change inlet face from (0 1 2 3) to (3 2 1 0); Change outlet face from (16 17 18 19) to (19 18 17 16); Reverse all individual faces on upperWall so each face list is reversed; Reverse all individual faces on lowerWall so each face list is reversed; Reverse all individual faces on leftWall so each face list is reversed; Reverse all individual faces on rightWall so each face list is reversed'}]}</rewrite_plan>

<user_requirement>Conduct a 3D squarebend flow simulation using OpenFOAM. The computational domain includes multiple regions, with vertices defined as inlet points at (-50, 25, 25) and (-50, 25, -25), outlet points at (-500, -75, 25) and (-500, -75, -25), and mid-bend points like (25, 0, 25) and (25, 0, -25). Follow the same geomtery as the tutorial. Use a start time of 0 seconds, an end time of 500 seconds, a time step of 1 second, and write results every 100 seconds. Use a massflow rate inlet of 0.5 with turbulentBL profile. Outlet is pressureInletOutletVelocity. Temperature has an inlet value of 1000. Pressure inlet has a value of 110000. use kepsilon turbulence model. Use rhoSimpleFoam solver. Use Pr value of 1.0.</user_requirement>

Please update OpenFOAM files according to rewrite_plan only. Only include files from rewrite_plan.target_files in your output.
